description: Satisficing detection rules to identify low-effort or disengaged response patterns. When thresholds are met, the engine flags the behavior and may inject engagement resets.

straightlining.description: Respondent selects the same scale value for 5 or more consecutive items on Likert or semantic differential scales

speed.description: Respondent completes a complex item (estimated >10 seconds) in under 2 seconds, indicating the item was not read

alternation.description: Respondent alternates between extreme scale values (e.g., 1,7,1,7) for 4 or more consecutive questions, indicating patterned non-engagement

engagement_reset_threshold.description: When cumulative satisficing flags reach or exceed this threshold, inject an engagement reset prompt

session_validity.description: Thresholds for determining whether the overall session data is usable

notes.flag_accumulation: Flags accumulate across all detection rules. A single response can trigger at most one flag from each rule type.
notes.engagement_reset: See deep-dive-triggers.json low_engagement_detector for the corresponding trigger rule.
notes.estimated_seconds: Each question item has an estimated_seconds field in the question bank indicating expected minimum completion time for engaged respondents.
